Class SettingsScreen

java.lang.Object
ui.BaseScreen
ui.SettingsScreen
public class SettingsScreen extends BaseScreen
Settings screen with three sub-sections: Categories, Security, and Cycle.

A secondary vertical nav bar on the left switches between the sections using a CardLayout.

  • Method Details

    • initComponents

      protected void initComponents()
      Initialises all UI components (fields, buttons, labels, etc.). Called before BaseScreen.initLayout().
      Specified by:
      initComponents in class BaseScreen

    • initLayout

      protected void initLayout()
      Arranges the initialised components into the BaseScreen.panel hierarchy. Called after BaseScreen.initComponents().
      Specified by:
      initLayout in class BaseScreen

    • createSubNavBar

      public JPanel createSubNavBar(String activeItem)
      Builds the secondary vertical nav bar for switching between settings sections.
      Parameters:
      activeItem - name of the initially active section
      Returns:
      the assembled sub-nav panel

    • buildSubNavBtn

      private JButton buildSubNavBtn(String text)
      Creates a styled secondary nav button.
      Parameters:
      text - button label
      Returns:
      the configured JButton

    • setActiveSubNav

      private void setActiveSubNav(JButton active)
      Highlights the active sub-nav button and resets the others.
      Parameters:
      active - the button to mark as active

    • buildSettingsRow

      private JPanel buildSettingsRow(String title, String subtitle, JComponent action)
      Builds a single settings row with a title, subtitle, and an action component.
      Parameters:
      title - primary label text
      subtitle - secondary description text
      action - the interactive component (button, checkbox, etc.)
      Returns:
      the assembled settings row panel

    • buildCategoriesPanel

      private JPanel buildCategoriesPanel()
      Builds the Categories sub-panel with a list of existing categories and controls to add or delete them.
      Returns:
      the categories panel

    • buildSecurityPanel

      private JPanel buildSecurityPanel()
      Builds the Security sub-panel with PIN enable toggle and change PIN row.
      Returns:
      the security panel

    • buildCyclePanel

      private JPanel buildCyclePanel()
      Builds the Cycle sub-panel with a reset cycle button.
      Returns:
      the cycle panel

    • onResetCycleClicked

      public void onResetCycleClicked()
      Handles the "Reset" button click in the Cycle panel. Asks for confirmation, resets the cycle, and navigates to the setup screen.

    • onEnablePinToggled

      public void onEnablePinToggled(boolean isEnabled)
      Handles the PIN enable/disable toggle. Prompts the user to set a PIN when enabling; clears the PIN when disabling.
      Parameters:
      isEnabled - true if the checkbox was just checked

    • onChangePinClicked

      public void onChangePinClicked()
      Handles the "Change PIN" button click. Prompts for a new 4-digit PIN and updates it if valid.
